compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
The grep-against-source inventory surfaced ANTI-PATTERN existence tests beyond the report's
list, each able to pass while the behavior is broken:
`test_applier_exceptions.py` (three `hasattr`-only importability tests that never raise the
error); `test_applier_reschedule.py::test_exit_reschedule_constant_is_distinct` (`==75`);
`test_fs_tools_extraction.py::test_fs_tools_exposes_the_shared_cluster` (7-symbol `hasattr`
sweep); `test_overlap_retrieve.py::test_field_weights_constant` (exact private
`_FIELD_WEIGHTS` values); `test_code_review_artifact.py::test_code_review_excluded_from_
jira_sync` (asserts `code_review` present/absent in reconciler source). VALIDATED.

## What
Replace each with a test that provokes the real behavior: trigger each exception path;
assert EXIT_RESCHEDULE via the reschedule path that returns it; prove the fs_tools cluster
via the existing identity/reuse seam; exercise ranking that `_FIELD_WEIGHTS` drives; run
`reconcile` and assert `code_review` tickets are excluded from Jira sync.

## Scope
- The five test files above.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each error class is raised by exercising the failing path (not `hasattr`).
- [ ] `EXIT_RESCHEDULE` and `_FIELD_WEIGHTS` are asserted via observable behavior, not
      constant values.
- [ ] `code_review` Jira exclusion is proven by running reconcile, not source grep.
- [ ] fs_tools reuse is proven by identity, not existence.
- [ ] `make test` green.

file_impact.reason: provoke error paths instead of hasattr importability checks
assert EXIT_RESCHEDULE via reschedule path, not constant value
prove fs_tools reuse by identity, not existence sweep
exercise ranking driven by _FIELD_WEIGHTS, not the constant values
prove code_review Jira exclusion via reconcile run, not source grep

compiled_state.title: Replace hasattr/constant existence tests with behavioral provocation
